wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Smartfolio project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as an enterprise-grade SaaS portfolio builder, its core value proposition, and key differentiators. Document the technology stack including Next.js 16, tRPC, Prisma, Better Auth, and Stripe integration. Describe the target audience and use cases. Include high-level architecture overview, main features summary, and business benefits. Provide context about the AI-native approach to portfolio creation and the drag-and-drop builder functionality. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and make content accessible to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system design documentation for Smartfolio. Describe the overall system architecture including the modular monolith pattern, system boundaries, and component relationships. Document the technology stack rationale including Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and external service integrations. Explain the system's scalability design, deployment topology, and infrastructure requirements. Include architectural diagrams showing system components, data flow, and integration points. Address performance considerations, security architecture, and cross-cutting concerns. Document the separation of concerns between frontend, backend, and database layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Portfolio Creation and Editing Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the portfolio creation and editing workflow. Document the step-by-step process of creating new portfolios, including initial setup, template selection, and basic customization. Explain the editing interface, real-time preview functionality, and draft management. Cover portfolio metadata management, branding options, and content organization. Include practical examples of portfolio CRUD operations, form validation, and error handling. Document the relationship between portfolios and user accounts, permission inheritance, and collaborative editing features. Address portfolio versioning, autosave functionality, and recovery mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Registration and Login
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for user registration and login functionality. Document the complete authentication workflow including email/password registration, login processes, password validation, and account verification. Explain the implementation of user registration hooks, form handling, and validation logic. Detail the login state management, session creation, and user profile initialization. Include practical examples of registration forms, login components, and error handling patterns. Address security considerations for password storage, validation rules, and account security measures. Provide guidance on customizing registration flows and handling edge cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stripe Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Stripe integration documentation for Smartfolio's billing system. Document the Stripe SDK configuration, API key management, and client/server-side integration patterns. Explain how Stripe customer objects are created and managed, payment method handling, and subscription object lifecycle. Include practical examples of Stripe checkout sessions, subscription creation, and payment confirmation flows. Document error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and fallback scenarios. Address Stripe dashboard configuration, webhook endpoint setup, and testing with Stripe CLI. Provide guidance for environment-specific configurations and production deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Natural Language Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the natural language processing capabilities in Smartfolio's AI system. Document the text generation workflows, prompt engineering patterns, and content quality controls. Explain the OpenAI API integration, model selection, and parameter tuning. Include practical examples of portfolio content generation from natural language descriptions, content refinement techniques, and error handling strategies. Address prompt optimization, content validation, and response streaming mechanisms. Provide guidance for customizing AI prompts and managing content generation costs.

wiki_catalogs.name: User API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for user management endpoints. Document all user-related tRPC procedures including user registration, login, profile management, account settings, and authentication workflows. For each endpoint, specify input/output schemas, authentication requirements, and error handling. Include practical examples of user CRUD operations, session management, and OAuth integration. Document user role-based access control, permission checking, and security considerations. Address common use cases like password reset, email verification, and account deletion. Provide guidance for client implementation patterns and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Smartfolio's UI component library. Document each reusable component including Button, Card, Dialog, Dropdown, and Input components. For each component, explain props interface, styling options, event handlers, and customization patterns. Include practical usage examples with code snippets showing different configurations and states. Document Tailwind CSS classes, responsive behavior, and accessibility features. Explain component composition patterns, integration with form validation, and state management. Address performance considerations, browser compatibility, and design system consistency. Provide guidelines for extending components and maintaining design tokens.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Container Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Smartfolio's Service Container architecture. Explain the singleton pattern implementation, dependency injection strategy, and service lifecycle management. Document the ServiceContainer class structure, initialization process, and service factory methods. Include practical examples of accessing services, configuration options, and environment variable integration. Address service instantiation patterns, memory management, and cleanup procedures. Provide guidance for extending the service container and adding new service dependencies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Smartfolio. Include detailed prerequisites (Node.js 18+, PostgreSQL, npm/pnpm), step-by-step installation instructions, environment configuration using .env.example, and initial setup procedures. Document development server startup, port configuration, and first-run verification. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues. Include code examples for environment variable configuration and basic project structure exploration. Address both local development and production deployment preparation. Make the content beginner-friendly while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed frontend architecture documentation for Smartfolio's Next.js 16 implementation. Explain the App Router structure, layout hierarchy, and component organization. Document the tRPC provider setup, global state management, and client-side data fetching patterns. Describe the UI component library architecture, custom hooks ecosystem, and reusable component patterns. Include the authentication layout system, marketing layout structure, and responsive design implementation. Address performance optimization strategies, code splitting approaches, and bundle size management. Document the integration between frontend components and backend services through tRPC.

wiki_catalogs.name: Section-Based Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the section-based content management system. Detail the different section types available, their properties, and configuration options. Explain the drag-and-drop interface for arranging sections, section reordering, and hierarchical organization. Document section-specific settings, content validation, and display options. Include examples of adding, editing, and removing sections. Cover section templates, reusable content blocks, and section duplication features. Address section permissions, conditional visibility, and responsive behavior across different devices.

wiki_catalogs.name: OAuth Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OAuth integration with external providers. Detail the implementation of Google and GitHub OAuth flows, including provider configuration, callback handling, and user data mapping. Document the OAuth hooks, state management, and token exchange processes. Explain the provider-specific configurations, scopes, and consent handling. Include practical examples of OAuth login buttons, redirect flows, and error handling for authentication failures. Address security considerations for OAuth tokens, state verification, and provider-specific limitations. Provide guidance for adding new OAuth providers and troubleshooting common integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Subscription Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed subscription management documentation covering subscription lifecycle, tier configuration, and state transitions. Document subscription creation, cancellation, upgrade/downgrade processes, and renewal handling. Explain subscription status tracking, grace periods, and subscription expiration logic. Include practical examples of subscription hooks usage, state management patterns, and user-facing subscription controls. Document subscription limits, feature gating, and access control based on subscription tiers. Address subscription analytics, revenue tracking, and usage-based billing calculations. Provide guidance for implementing custom subscription workflows and managing subscription conflicts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voice Input Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for voice input processing in Smartfolio's AI system. Detail the speech-to-text conversion workflow, audio recording capabilities, and real-time voice processing. Document the microphone integration, audio format handling, and transcription quality optimization. Include practical examples of voice-powered content creation, voice command interpretation, and audio file processing. Address voice input permissions, browser compatibility, noise cancellation, and fallback text input methods. Provide guidance for customizing voice recognition parameters and handling various audio formats.

wiki_catalogs.name: Portfolio API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for portfolio management endpoints. Document all portfolio-related tRPC procedures including portfolio creation, editing, publishing, and deletion. Cover portfolio section management, template operations, and content organization. For each endpoint, specify input/output schemas, validation rules, and business logic constraints. Include practical examples of portfolio workflows, section manipulation, and publishing processes. Document portfolio sharing, collaboration features, and access control mechanisms. Address common scenarios like portfolio duplication, template application, and bulk operations. Provide guidance for portfolio versioning and content migration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Smartfolio's layout components system. Document AuthLayout and MarketingLayout components, explaining their purpose, props, and usage scenarios. Detail the layout structure, navigation patterns, and content organization for each layout type. Include examples of how layouts integrate with Next.js App Router and handle different user contexts. Document responsive design implementation, mobile-first approach, and accessibility features. Explain layout composition patterns, header/footer configurations, and content area management. Address SSR considerations, SEO optimization, and performance implications. Provide guidelines for creating custom layouts and maintaining consistent user experience across different pages.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Service Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Smartfolio's AI Service implementation. Document the AIService class architecture, OpenAI integration patterns, and content generation workflows. Explain AI provider abstraction, model configuration, and response processing. Include practical examples of AI content generation, streaming responses, and error handling. Document rate limiting integration, cost management, and performance optimization. Address AI provider switching, custom model configurations, and monitoring capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for Smartfolio's system design. Describe the high-level architecture including the modular monolith pattern, layer separation (presentation, business logic, data), and component interactions. Document the Next.js App Router architecture, tRPC integration patterns, and authentication middleware flow. Explain the technology choices justification including React 19, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and external service integrations. Include system boundary diagrams, data flow architecture, and integration patterns. Address scalability considerations, security architecture, and deployment topology. Document the middleware pipeline, request processing flow, and cross-cutting concerns implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed backend architecture documentation for Smartfolio's server-side implementation. Explain the tRPC router organization, procedure calling patterns, and middleware pipeline. Document the service layer architecture, business logic implementation, and data access patterns. Describe the authentication middleware flow, request processing pipeline, and error handling strategies. Include the Prisma ORM integration, database interaction patterns, and transaction management. Address the separation between API endpoints, business logic services, and data persistence. Document the caller pattern for procedure invocation and service dependency injection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template System and Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the template system architecture and customization capabilities. Explain the template creation process, template categories, and selection criteria. Detail the customization options available for each template, including color schemes, typography, layout variations, and branding elements. Cover template inheritance, modification workflows, and template sharing between users. Include examples of creating custom templates, modifying existing ones, and applying templates to portfolios. Document template validation, preview functionality, and performance considerations. Address template versioning, backup strategies, and restoration processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for session management and persistence. Document the session lifecycle, cookie handling, and token refresh mechanisms. Explain the implementation of session hooks, client-side authentication state, and server-side session validation. Detail the middleware-based session checking, automatic logout on token expiration, and session renewal processes. Include practical examples of session-aware components, protected route handling, and authentication state synchronization. Address security considerations for session storage, CSRF protection, and session hijacking prevention. Provide guidance on customizing session behavior and handling concurrent sessions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed payment processing documentation covering payment flow implementation, transaction handling, and payment verification. Document payment method acceptance, currency conversion, and tax calculation integration. Explain payment failure scenarios, retry logic, and payment recovery mechanisms. Include practical examples of payment intent creation, confirmation handling, and refund processing. Document PCI compliance considerations, secure payment storage, and payment method tokenization. Address international payment support, multi-currency handling, and regional payment method preferences. Provide guidance for implementing custom payment processors and handling edge cases in payment workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Attachment Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for file attachment processing capabilities within Smartfolio's AI system. Document the file upload workflow, supported file formats, and content extraction techniques. Explain image analysis, document processing, and file metadata handling. Include practical examples of AI-powered content generation from uploaded files, file-based content analysis, and multi-modal input processing. Address file size limitations, security scanning, temporary storage management, and cleanup procedures. Provide guidance for extending supported file types and optimizing file processing performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Generation API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for AI content generation endpoints. Document all AI-related tRPC procedures including content generation, voice processing, file attachment handling, and real-time streaming. For each endpoint, specify input/output schemas, AI provider configurations, and streaming response formats. Include practical examples of natural language processing, voice-to-text conversion, and file analysis workflows. Document AI generation parameters, quality controls, and content filtering. Address real-time streaming patterns, connection management, and error recovery strategies. Provide guidance for AI provider switching, rate limiting, and cost optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Hooks
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Smartfolio's custom React hooks library. Document each hook including useClickOutside, useDebounce, useLocalStorage, and useMediaQuery. For each hook, explain the problem it solves, parameters, return values, and usage patterns. Include practical examples showing real-world scenarios and integration with components. Document hook dependencies, performance implications, and best practices for usage. Explain state management patterns, side effects handling, and cleanup procedures. Address hook composition, testing strategies, and debugging techniques. Provide guidelines for creating new custom hooks and maintaining consistency across the codebase. Include performance optimization tips and common pitfalls to avoid.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Service Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Smartfolio's Email Service implementation. Document the EmailService class structure, SMTP configuration, and email delivery workflows. Explain template rendering, email queuing, and delivery tracking. Include practical examples of sending transactional emails, email verification processes, and notification systems. Document SMTP provider integration, authentication methods, and security considerations. Address email formatting, attachments, and bulk email processing capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication system documentation for Smartfolio. Detail the Better Auth integration, user registration and login workflows, OAuth implementation with Google and GitHub, session management, and role-based access control. Document authentication hooks, utility functions, and type definitions. Explain the middleware-based authentication flow, protected route implementation, and security best practices. Include practical examples of authentication state management, token handling, and user session persistence. Address common authentication scenarios, error handling, and troubleshooting authentication issues. Provide guidance for extending authentication features and integrating additional providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive data flow architecture documentation for Smartfolio. Explain the complete data pathway from user input through UI components, tRPC procedures, business services, and database persistence. Document the request-response cycle, real-time data streaming patterns, and asynchronous operation handling. wiki_catalogs.name: Publishing and Deployment Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the portfolio publishing and deployment workflow. Explain the publishing process, pre-publish validation checks, and quality assurance steps. wiki_catalogs.name: Access Control and Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for access control and authorization mechanisms. Detail the role-based access control (RBAC) implementation, permission checking, and route protection strategies. Explain the implementation of protected routes, API endpoint authorization, and dynamic permission checks. Include practical examples of access control decorators, conditional rendering based on permissions, and permission-based navigation. Address security considerations for authorization bypass attempts, permission escalation, and audit logging. wiki_catalogs.name: Webhook Handling

Document webhook endpoint configuration, signature verification, and event payload parsing. Explain subscription event handling, payment success/failure notifications, and invoice generation events. Include practical examples of webhook processing functions, event deduplication, and error handling strategies. Document webhook retry mechanisms, dead letter queues, and monitoring approaches. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Smartfolio: AI-Native Portfolio Generator - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## Project Introduction

Smartfolio is an innovative AI-native portfolio generator built with Next.js that enables users to create professional portfolios through natural language prompts. The platform focuses on a streamlined workspace-first UX model, eliminating traditional dashboard complexity in favor of a single-product experience centered around the AI workspace.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **AI-Powered Content Creation**: Generate complete portfolio content through conversational AI interactions
- **Real-Time Collaboration**: Provide live preview rendering with iterative refinement capabilities  
- **Monetization Strategy**: Implement subscription-based billing with tiered usage limits
- **Developer Experience**: Offer a clean, modular architecture with TypeScript-first development

### Target Audience

- Job seekers seeking professional portfolio presentation
- Freelancers building personal brands
- Developers showcasing technical skills through interactive portfolios
- Creative professionals presenting visual work

## Technical Architecture

Smartfolio employs a modern full-stack architecture built on Next.js App Router with a clear separation between frontend and backend concerns.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[tRPC API Layer]
    C --> D[Server Routers]
    D --> E[Services Layer]
    E --> F[Database Prisma]
    
    G[AI Service] --> E
    H[Billing Service] --> E
    I[Storage Service] --> E
    
    F --> J[PostgreSQL]
    K[AWS S3] --> L[File Storage]
    M[Stripe API] --> N[Pricing & Billing]
    O[OpenAI API] --> G
```

### Component Breakdown

The application follows a modular architecture with distinct layers:

**Frontend Layer**: React 19 with Next.js App Router, utilizing TypeScript for type safety and component composition

**API Layer**: tRPC provides type-safe RPC calls between client and server, eliminating boilerplate code

**Business Logic**: Service layer encapsulates complex operations like AI generation, billing processing, and file management

**Data Layer**: Prisma ORM with PostgreSQL for structured data persistence

### Design Patterns

- **Module Pattern**: Feature-scoped modules (auth, portfolio, ai, billing) with barrel exports
- **Repository Pattern**: Prisma models abstract database operations
- **Service Container**: Singleton pattern for service management
- **Middleware Pattern**: Request processing pipeline with authentication and rate limiting

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Shell**: `app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with global styling and tRPC provider setup

**Home Page**: `app/page.tsx` - Landing page featuring voice input, file attachment, and authentication modal

**API Endpoints**: `app/api/` - Next.js API routes for authentication, tRPC, and Stripe webhooks

### Core Modules

**Authentication Module** (`modules/auth/`): Handles user sessions, OAuth flows, and access control using Better Auth

**Portfolio Management** (`modules/portfolio/`): CRUD operations for portfolio creation, editing, and publishing workflows

**AI Generation** (`modules/ai/`): Natural language processing integration with OpenAI for content generation

**Billing System** (`modules/billing/`): Stripe integration for subscription management and payment processing

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Variables**: `.env` and `.env.example` for secure configuration management

**Database Schema**: `prisma/schema.prisma` defines comprehensive data models for users, portfolios, subscriptions, and analytics

**Build Configuration**: `next.config.ts`, `tsconfig.json`, and `postcss.config.mjs` for development and production builds

### External Dependencies

Key technologies include:
- **Next.js 16.x**: Modern React framework with App Router
- **tRPC 11.x**: Type-safe API layer with automatic client generation
- **Better Auth 1.4.x**: Authentication solution with OAuth support
- **Prisma 6.x**: Database ORM with PostgreSQL support
- **Stripe 20.x**: Payment processing and subscription management
- **OpenAI SDK**: AI content generation capabilities

### Integration Points

**AWS S3 Integration**: File upload and storage management for portfolio assets

**Stripe Webhooks**: Automated subscription lifecycle management and payment processing

**OpenAI API**: Structured content generation for portfolio sections and metadata

**Nodemailer**: Transactional email notifications for account and billing events

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Smartfolio App] --> B[Stripe API]
    A --> C[AWS S3]
    A --> D[OpenAI API]
    A --> E[Nodemailer]
    
    B --> F[Payment Processing]
    C --> G[Asset Storage]
    D --> H[Content Generation]
    E --> I[Email Delivery]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Workspace Interface**: Single-page application focused on AI-driven portfolio creation with real-time preview

**Multi-Modal Input**: Supports text prompts, voice input, and file attachments for comprehensive content creation

**Template System**: Predefined portfolio templates with customization options for different professional domains

**Publishing Workflow**: One-click portfolio publishing with SEO optimization and custom domain support

**Usage Analytics**: Built-in analytics tracking for published portfolios including visitor metrics and engagement data

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-Time Streaming**: AI generation processes stream content updates to provide immediate feedback during creation

**Iterative Refinement**: Users can refine existing portfolios through follow-up prompts without starting over

**Cross-Platform Compatibility**: Responsive design works across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices

**Security Framework**: Multi-layered security including authentication, authorization, rate limiting, and input validation

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Landing
    Landing --> AuthModal: User clicks Get Started
    AuthModal --> Workspace: Login/Signup Complete
    
    Workspace --> PromptInput: Ready for AI Generation
    PromptInput --> AIGeneration: Submit Prompt
    AIGeneration --> LivePreview: Generation Started
    LivePreview --> PortfolioReview: Generation Complete
    
    PortfolioReview --> Publishing: User clicks Publish
    Publishing --> PublishedPortfolio: Deployment Complete
    PublishedPortfolio --> [*]: Portfolio Live
    
    Workspace --> PortfolioReview: Existing Portfolio
    PortfolioReview --> AIGeneration: Refine Content
```

The architecture demonstrates a well-thought-out approach to AI-native application development, prioritizing user experience while maintaining technical excellence through modular design and robust backend infrastructure.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [page.tsx](app/page.tsx)
- [_app.ts](server/routers/_app.ts)
- [schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [middleware.ts](middleware.ts)
- [trpc-provider.tsx](lib/trpc-provider.tsx)

</blog>
